key_insight: Active learning can dramatically reduce the data needed to discover governing equations by repeatedly sampling the most informative regions instead of collecting points randomly.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient system identification for costly experimental or simulation settings in ODEs and PDEs, especially when measurements are scarce.
method: Build on SINDy for sparse dynamics discovery.
Use E-SINDy to estimate epistemic uncertainty.
Iteratively sample regions predicted to be most informative for model identification.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on the Lorenz system and on Burgers' and Kuramoto-Sivashinsky PDEs across different data budgets and noise levels, and it recovered the dynamics with fewer samples than random sampling.
watch_item: Performance depends on uncertainty estimates being reliable; noisy data or poorly captured dynamics could misguide the active sampling loop.
paper_title: How Low Can You Go? Active Learning for Sparse Model Discovery in the Ultra-Low-Data Limit
